problem.instruction: Read the passage in <A> and the master plan in <B>, and follow the directions.
problem.passage:
<A>
Ms. Kim, a high school English teacher, attended an ICT workshop for English teachers. There she learned how to select digital tools that best fit her students' needs and use them appropriately. Below is the list of principles she took note of during the workshop.

<Guiding Principles for Using Digital Tools>
① Encourage students to independently explore and discover language rules.
② Support learners with diverse learning styles (e.g., auditory styles, visual styles).
③ Teach digital ethics (e.g., citing properly).
④ Assess student achievement and provide individualized feedback.

<B>
Draft of the Master Plan

Unit: Save the Earth

Objectives:
  Students will be able to:
  · identify the main idea and details of a text or discourse
  · write an opinion using textual and non-textual elements
  · use digital tools responsibly and ethically

| Period | Contents | Technology |
|--------|----------|------------|
| 1st | · Introduce the topic, 'Save the Earth' | Online videos / Internet search engines |
|     | · Watch a video on environmental problems | |
|     | · Teach how to use the Internet properly (e.g., locating information, sourcing, netiquette) | |
| 2nd | · Make predictions about a text using titles and pictures | Word cloud generator to visualize key concepts |
|     | · Read the passage, 'Plastic Pollution' | |
|     | · Identify key words and main ideas | |
| 3rd | · Provide definitions of new words | PPT slides |
|     | · Teach grammar points explicitly using drills | |
| … | … | … |
| 7th | · Brainstorm ideas to solve environmental problems and share in groups | Online collaborative writing platform |
|     | · In groups, create a 'Save the Earth' poster | |
| 8th | · Exhibit groups' posters on the walls | Online quiz platform |
|     | · Conduct a team-based quiz and provide comments to groups | |

Based on <A>, identify the TWO guiding principles that Ms. Kim does NOT conform to in her lessons in <B>. Then, explain your answers, respectively, with evidence from <B>.

steps.label: 범위 설정 — 4개 원칙 확인
steps.explanation:
<A>의 디지털 도구 활용 4원칙:
① 학생이 언어 규칙을 independently explore하고 discover하도록 장려 (discovery learning)
② 다양한 학습 스타일 지원 (auditory, visual 등)
③ 디지털 윤리 교육 (인용 등)
④ 학생 성취를 평가하고 individualized feedback 제공
<B>의 각 수업 기간을 위 원칙과 대조하여 위반 사례를 찾는 과제.

steps.label: 이론↔지문 대조 — 각 기간 검토
steps.explanation:
1st: 인터넷 사용법 교육 → 원칙 ③(디지털 윤리) 부합. ✓
2nd: Word cloud로 개념 시각화, 읽기 활동 → 원칙 ②(다양한 학습 스타일) 부합. ✓
3rd: 단어 정의 직접 제공 + drills로 명시적 문법 지도 → ① 위반 후보 ✓
7th: 협력 글쓰기 플랫폼 사용, 그룹 포스터 제작 → 특별한 위반 없음. ✓
8th: 팀 기반 퀴즈 + 그룹 코멘트 제공 → ④ 위반 후보 ✓

steps.label: Principle ① 불준수 — 3rd period
steps.explanation: Principle ①은 학생이 언어 규칙을 independently explore and discover하도록 장려하는 것이다(discovery learning). 그러나 3rd period에서 Ms. Kim은 PPT slides로 새 단어 정의를 직접 제공하고 drills를 통해 문법을 explicitly 지도했다. 이는 교사 주도의 직접 교수(explicit instruction)로, 학생의 자율 탐구를 요구하는 ①에 어긋난다.

steps.label: Principle ④ 불준수 — 8th period
steps.explanation: Principle ④는 개별 학생 성취를 평가하고 individualized feedback을 제공하는 것이다. 그러나 8th period에서 Ms. Kim은 team-based quiz를 실시하고 그룹 전체에 대한 코멘트(comments to groups)만 제공했다. 이는 개인별 성취 평가 및 individualized feedback 요건을 충족하지 못하므로 ④에 어긋난다.

steps.label: 전략 메모 — 함정 구별
steps.explanation:
함정 주의:
① 원칙 ①은 결과적으로 문법을 가르치는 것이 문제가 아니라, explicitly/직접 교수 방식이 문제. 'drills'와 '직접 정의 제공'이 핵심 근거.
② 원칙 ④에서 quiz 자체가 문제가 아니라 team-based(집단 단위)로 진행하고 comments도 groups에게만 제공된다는 점이 핵심. 개인별 피드백 부재가 이탈 근거.
③ 원칙 ②는 2nd period의 Word cloud(시각 자료)가 이미 충족. ③은 1st period가 충족. 혼동 주의.

key_terms.definition_en: An instructional approach, rooted in Bruner (1961), in which learners actively construct knowledge by exploring and finding patterns on their own rather than receiving direct instruction.
key_terms.definition_ko: 교사의 직접 설명 대신 학습자가 스스로 탐구·발견을 통해 능동적으로 지식을 구성하는 교수 접근법 (Bruner 1961).
key_terms.exam_usage: Principle ①: 학생이 언어 규칙을 independently explore하고 discover하도록 장려 — 3rd period 명시적 문법 드릴과 대비

key_terms.definition_en: Feedback tailored to each individual learner's specific performance, needs, or progress, as distinguished from group-level or generic feedback.
key_terms.definition_ko: 집단 전체가 아닌 각 학습자 개인의 수행, 필요, 진도에 맞춰 제공되는 피드백. 집단 코멘트와 구별됨.
key_terms.exam_usage: Principle ④: 8th period에서 team-based quiz + comments to groups만 제공 — individualized feedback 부재

model_answer.essay_content: Principles ① and ④ are not conformed to. First, in the 3rd period, Ms. Kim teaches grammar points explicitly using drills, which contradicts principle ①, which calls for students to independently explore and discover language rules. Second, in the 8th period, she conducts a team-based quiz and provides comments to groups, rather than assessing individual achievement and offering individualized feedback as required by principle ④.

scoring_criteria.criterion: 근거: 3rd period에서 문법을 drills로 명시적 지도 (explicit teaching, not discovery)
근거: 8th period에서 팀 기반 퀴즈 + 그룹 코멘트 (not individualized feedback)

variants.label: 답안 A
variants.content: Principles 1 and 4 are not conformed to. In the 3rd period, the teacher provides definitions and teaches grammar points explicitly using drills rather than discovery learning. In the 8th period, the teacher conducts a team-based quiz and provides comments to groups rather than individual feedback.

variants.label: 답안 B
variants.content: Ms. Kim does not conform to principles ① and ④. First, she explicitly teaches grammar points through drills instead of encouraging students to independently explore and discover language rules. Additionally, she conducts a team-based quiz and provides comments to groups rather than assessing individual performance and offering individualized feedback.

references.author: Bruner, J. S.
references.title: The Act of Discovery
references.excerpt: The hypothesis that discovery learning — where the learner constructs knowledge through guided exploration rather than reception of explicit information — leads to better retention and transfer. When the learner discovers a principle independently, it is more readily available for later application than when the same principle is presented through direct instruction.

references.author: Black, P. & Wiliam, D.
references.title: Inside the Black Box: Raising Standards Through Classroom Assessment
references.excerpt: Effective formative assessment requires that feedback be individualized feedback — targeted at the specific learner's understanding and misconceptions — rather than generic group-level comment. When teachers provide comments only to the class as a whole, students cannot identify what they personally need to do to improve, and the feedback loses much of its formative power.

source.concept_explanation:
ICT 통합 수업에서 디지털 도구 활용 4원칙:

① discovery learning (Bruner 1961) 기반: 학생이 언어 규칙을 independently explore하고 discover하도록 유도. 3rd period의 explicit grammar drills는 교사 주도의 직접 교수로 이 원칙과 배치된다.
② 다양한 학습 스타일 지원: auditory·visual 등 다양한 양식 제공.
③ 디지털 윤리 교육: 인용, 출처 표기, 네티켓 등.
④ 개별 평가 + individualized feedback: 각 학습자 개인의 성취에 맞춤화된 피드백 제공. 8th period의 team-based quiz와 group comments는 집단 단위 평가로, individualized feedback 원칙을 충족하지 못한다.
